description: A referendum on April 16, 2017, in Turkey that proposed changing the parliamentary system to an executive presidency. The 'Yes' side won with 51.4% of the vote, amidst international concerns regarding democratic standards.

key_events.description: Official campaign launch featuring intense rhetoric from the ruling AKP party.
Diplomatic row erupts after European countries ban Turkish ministers from campaigning abroad.
Economic anxiety dampens 'Yes' campaign momentum temporarily.
High turnout drives last-minute consolidation among nationalist voters.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling high-stakes referendums with significant 'undecided' blocks late in the cycle.
